2024-07-12.thesis: ADVANCE Act signed into law, streamlining NRC small-modular-reactor approvals
2024-07-12.exit_case: ADVANCE Act was signed into law ~July 9, 2024 — catalyst fully resolved before this entry date.
2024-07-12.assessment: This position was entered on July 12, but the catalyst (ADVANCE Act signed into law) had already fired by July 9-10 per the news record. The bill-signing is a one-time, irreversible event — there is no second signing, no pending vote, no approval still outstanding. The price reaction was instantaneous and is documented. Holding now means holding on theme/sentiment, not on an unresolved catalyst, which violates the entry logic. The honest call is: this trade was entered after the edge was already gone.
2024-07-12.exit_advice: Exit immediately. The specific catalyst — ADVANCE Act signing — occurred on or around July 9-10, 2024, confirmed by Motley Fool coverage showing same-day price spikes in SMR/NuScale, Cameco, and Energy Fuels. The discrete legislative event is done, publicly known, and already priced. No pending resolution remains; only residual theme/momentum, which does not sustain a thesis_live position.

2024-08-02.thesis: PJM capacity auction prices jumped ninefold, boosting nuclear revenue outlook
2024-08-02.exit_case: Auction results are already public and CEG/VST already soared August 1 — immediate catalyst is priced in day one.
2024-08-02.assessment: Week 1. The PJM 2025/2026 Base Residual Auction cleared at roughly $269/MW-day, a ~9x jump from the prior year's ~$28/MW-day. CEG, as the largest nuclear fleet owner in PJM, is the clearest beneficiary — capacity revenue is essentially riskless contracted income layered on top of energy and ancillary markets. The market reacted sharply on August 1, but sell-side earnings models, forward EPS estimates, and management guidance (next earnings call) have not yet been updated to reflect the new capacity price environment. The re-rating thesis is alive: (1) analysts must revise FY2026+ EPS upward once capacity prices are formally incorporated; (2) data-center / AI load-growth narrative amplifies the scarcity premium for always-on baseload; (3) potential nuclear policy tailwinds (IRA production tax credits, license extensions) are additive. The exit case is honest — the singular catalyst announcement is in the public domain — but the thesis depends on the DURATION of elevated capacity prices and their translation into reported earnings, which is a 12-24 month process. CEG remains the cleanest pure-play vehicle given its ~21 GW nuclear fleet and heavy PJM footprint. No reason to switch vehicles.
2024-08-02.exit_advice: Hold. The discrete auction announcement is public, but the multi-year revenue ratchet from the new ~$269/MW-day clearing price (vs ~$28 prior year) has not yet flowed through to earnings revisions, forward guidance, or actual capacity-payment receipts for the 2025/2026 delivery year. The first-day pop reflects awareness, not full re-rating.

2024-08-09.thesis: PJM capacity auction prices jumped ninefold, boosting nuclear revenue outlook
2024-08-09.exit_case: PJM auction cleared August 1 and is fully public; no new discrete catalyst remains pending this week.
2024-08-09.assessment: No change in fundamentals this week. The sole news item is about Chinese CPI data — entirely irrelevant to CEG or PJM capacity markets. The Week 1 journal acknowledged the auction result was already public but argued the re-rating process would take 12-24 months; that argument describes a theme/process, not a pending discrete catalyst. Re-reading the full journal under the strict framework definition — 'the specific catalyst you entered on already OCCURRED / passed / closed' — the ninefold price jump happened August 1 and is unambiguously resolved. Thesis_live must follow to false. CEG remains the cleanest vehicle in the known set if a new catalyst materialises, but no position is warranted now.
2024-08-09.exit_advice: Exit. The fixed catalyst — PJM auction prices jumping ninefold — is a completed, fully-public event that occurred on August 1, before entry. CEG's sharp move on that day reflects initial pricing of the news. The downstream re-rating story (analyst EPS revisions, earnings translation over 12-24 months) is a durable theme, not a discrete pending catalyst, and does not satisfy the threshold to keep thesis_live=true under the framework rules. This week's news contains zero CEG- or nuclear-relevant information, providing no new catalyst to restart the clock. The intellectually honest call is to exit and re-enter only if a new discrete pending catalyst emerges (e.g., a fresh PJM auction, a specific policy vote, a power-purchase agreement announcement).

2024-08-16.thesis: WHO declared mpox global health emergency, driving TPOXX procurement
2024-08-16.exit_case: WHO declaration already occurred and is fully public — SIGA surged immediately; announcement edge is gone.
2024-08-16.assessment: First week. WHO declared Clade Ib mpox a PHEIC on Aug 14, triggering sharp moves in SIGA and peers. The declaration itself is now priced into the initial spike. The durable, still-pending leg of the thesis is downstream procurement: BARDA stockpile orders, WHO/CEPI emergency purchasing, and affected-nation bilateral deals for TPOXX — none of which have been announced. SIGA holds FDA approval for the only antiviral specifically indicated for smallpox/mpox (tecovirimat), giving it a monopoly position on the treatment side. Vaccine plays (Bavarian Nordic) are noisy co-movers but dilute the pure TPOXX-procurement story. Key watch: any BARDA contract modification or international tender referencing tecovirimat. Outbreak severity and geographic spread of Clade Ib will set the ceiling on procurement size. Early in the arc; procurement pipeline is the next discrete catalyst.
2024-08-16.exit_advice: Hold while TPOXX procurement contracts remain unannounced; exit on a large signed contract award or credible outbreak-containment signal.

2024-08-23.thesis: WHO declared mpox global health emergency, driving TPOXX procurement
2024-08-23.exit_case: WHO PHEIC declaration (Aug 14) is public and fully priced; 10 days since entry with zero procurement contracts announced.
2024-08-23.assessment: Week 2. This week's news feed contains zero mpox- or SIGA-relevant items — entirely unrelated market noise. The WHO Clade Ib PHEIC remains in force; no BARDA stockpile order, no WHO emergency-use procurement framework, and no bilateral government deal for tecovirimat has been announced. The thesis is intact but still waiting for its discrete confirming event. The absence of procurement news is neutral rather than bearish — government stockpile cycles are slow, and the outbreak in DRC/central Africa is still actively spreading, maintaining the political pressure that precedes procurement decisions. SIGA retains its monopoly position as the only FDA-approved antiviral for mpox. No change to vehicle; SIGA remains the cleanest pure-play. Key watches remain: any BARDA contract modification (USAJOBS / USASpending), WHO Secretariat emergency purchase announcement, or an affected-nation bilateral deal citing tecovirimat. Each week without a procurement signal modestly erodes the probability of a large imminent order, so the hold is contingent on any of those triggers appearing in the next few weeks.
2024-08-23.exit_advice: Hold. The procurement leg — BARDA contract modification, WHO/CEPI tender, bilateral TPOXX orders — remains entirely unannounced. No resolution yet.

2024-08-30.thesis: WHO declared mpox global health emergency, driving TPOXX procurement
2024-08-30.exit_case: Three weeks post-PHEIC, zero procurement signals; initial spike priced, thesis eroding with each silent week.
2024-08-30.assessment: Week 3 — third consecutive week with zero mpox- or SIGA-specific news in the feed; all items are unrelated general healthcare or market noise. The WHO Clade Ib PHEIC declared Aug 14 remains in force and has not been lifted, so the political/regulatory pressure that precedes stockpile decisions is still present. However, the thesis is accumulating time risk: three weeks without a BARDA modification (USASpending), WHO Secretariat emergency-purchase framework, or bilateral government tender referencing tecovirimat is not fatal, but each silent week compresses the probability of an imminent large order. Government procurement cycles typically span 4–12 weeks after an emergency declaration; we are now inside that window but have seen no signal. Clade Ib outbreak geography (DRC, eastern Africa) continues to evolve and has not reversed, which sustains the humanitarian urgency that motivates procurement. SIGA retains its monopoly position as the only FDA-approved mpox antiviral. Vehicle unchanged — SIGA is still the cleanest pure-play on TPOXX procurement. Key watch: any USASpending award or modification to SIGA's existing BARDA contract (HHSO100201600011C or successor), WHO emergency-use tender, or CDC/HHS public statement on stockpile needs.
2024-08-30.exit_advice: Hold, but set a hard deadline: if no BARDA contract modification or WHO/bilateral procurement announcement appears within ~3 more weeks, treat time-decay as a soft exit signal and reduce exposure.

2024-09-06.thesis: WHO declared mpox global health emergency, driving TPOXX procurement
2024-09-06.exit_case: Four consecutive silent weeks post-PHEIC; governments appear to be bypassing TPOXX stockpiling entirely, time-decaying the procurement trigger.
2024-09-06.assessment: Week 4 — fourth consecutive week with zero mpox- or SIGA-specific news in the feed; all items are unrelated general healthcare or macro noise. The WHO Clade Ib PHEIC (declared Aug 14) technically remains in force and has not been lifted, so the legal/political precondition for emergency procurement still exists. The Clade Ib outbreak in DRC and eastern Africa has not reversed. However, the thesis is now under serious time pressure: we are deep inside the 4–12 week post-declaration procurement window that government stockpile cycles normally occupy, and not one confirming signal has appeared — no USASpending modification to SIGA's BARDA contract, no WHO emergency-use purchase framework, no CDC/HHS public statement on TPOXX stockpile needs, no bilateral tender from an affected nation. Each silent week compresses the implied probability that a large imminent order is forthcoming. The procurement catalyst is still technically pending (not denied, not canceled), which keeps thesis_live=true, but the hold rationale is thin. SIGA remains the only FDA-approved mpox antiviral and the cleanest vehicle if procurement materialises; no vehicle change warranted. Key watch: any USASpending award or contract modification referencing SIGA/tecovirimat, or a WHO/HHS public statement on TPOXX stockpile requirements.
2024-09-06.exit_advice: Thesis is on a very short leash. Four weeks inside the 4–12 week post-emergency procurement window with zero signal from USASpending, BARDA, WHO Secretariat, or any bilateral tender is materially eroding probability of an imminent large order. If no procurement award or contract modification surfaces within the next 1–2 weeks, exit SIGA — the window will have effectively closed without delivering the discrete catalyst.

2024-09-13.thesis: WHO declared mpox global health emergency, driving TPOXX procurement
2024-09-13.exit_case: Five weeks, zero BARDA/WHO/bilateral procurement signals; PHEIC's procurement-driving thesis has fully decayed.
2024-09-13.assessment: Week 5: fifth consecutive week with zero mpox- or SIGA-specific news; both feed items are entirely unrelated (tanker markets, California EV program). We are now 5 weeks past the Aug 14 WHO PHEIC declaration — well inside, and past the midpoint of, the typical 4–12 week government stockpile cycle — and not one procurement signal has appeared: no USASpending modification to SIGA's BARDA contract, no WHO emergency-use purchase framework, no HHS/CDC public statement on TPOXX stockpile needs, no bilateral government tender. The WHO PHEIC has not been formally lifted (catalyst_resolved=false), but the specific thesis — that the declaration would drive imminent TPOXX procurement — has clearly failed to activate over five silent weeks. The absence of any signal across five full procurement-cycle weeks is material negative evidence, not neutral noise. The original edge (being positioned ahead of procurement announcements) is gone. Thesis_live set to false; recommend exit.
2024-09-13.exit_advice: EXIT — close SIGA. Five consecutive weeks inside the expected 4–12 week post-declaration procurement window with zero confirming signals is sufficient evidence the thesis has decayed. The PHEIC is technically still in force (catalyst_resolved=false), but that no longer constitutes a live edge.
